hadiths.chapter: 1. Introduction
hadiths.ar: بِسْمِ اللّهِ الرَّحْمنِ الرَّحِيمِ الحمد لله الواحد القديم الأزلي الذي لا يوصف بحد و لا نهاية و لا تَأْخُذُهُ سِنَةٌ وَ لا نَوْمٌ الذي لا ابتداء لكونه و لا غاية لبقائه الدال على وجوده بخلقه و بإحداث خلقه على أزليته و بأشباههم على أن لا شبه له المستشهد بآياته على قدرته الممتنعة من الصفات ذاته و من الأبصار رؤيته و من الأوهام الإحاطة به الذي لَيْسَ كَمِثْلِهِ شَيْءٌ وَ هُوَ السَّمِيعُ الْبَصِيرُ العليم و أشهد أن لا إله إلا الله وحده لا شريك له الذي وعد على طاعته ثوابه و على معصيته عقابه و أشهد أن محمدا عبده و رسوله أرسله بكتاب فصله و حكمة فأيده- لا يَأْتِيهِ الْباطِلُ مِنْ بَيْنِ يَدَيْهِ وَ لا مِنْ خَلْفِهِ تَنْزِيلٌ مِنْ حَكِيمٍ حَمِيدٍ و أشهد أن أمير المؤمنين علي بن أبي طالب و الأئمة الطاهرين من ولده حجج الله على خلقه بعد انقضاء وحيه و أشهد أن شيعتهم و مواليهم المتبعين لسنتهم و طريقتهم على صراط مستقيم و أنهم الْمُؤْمِنُونَ حَقًّا و أن الذين خالفوهم و حادوا عن طريقتهم و تركوا أمرهم و لم يستنوا بسنتهم- عَنِ الصِّراطِ لَناكِبُونَ و قد ظلوا السبيل أسأل الله أن يثبتنا على دينهم و موالاتهم و محبتهم و أن لا يزيغ قلوبنا بعد إذ هدانا و أن يهب لنا من لدنه رحمة إنه هو الوهاب قال محمد بن علي بن الحسين بن موسى بن بابويه القمي (رحمه الله) إن الذي دعاني إلى تأليف كتابي هذا- - مَا رُوِيَ عَنِ النَّبِيِّ (ص) أَنَّهُ قَالَ الدَّالُّ عَلَى الْخَيْرِ كَفَاعِلِهِ و سميته كتاب ثواب الأعمال و أرجو أن لا يحرمني الله ثواب ذلك فما أردت من تصنيفه إلا الرغبة في ثواب الله و ابتغاء مرضاته سبحانه و لا أردت بما تكلفته غير ذلك و لا حول و لا قوة إلا بالله و هو حسبنا وَ نِعْمَ الْوَكِيلُ
hadiths.en: 1.  In the name of God, most Gracious, most Compassionate Praise be to Allah the One, the Eternal and the Ever living. One Whose limits and ends could not be imagined. Neither slumber overtakes Him nor sleep, the One Whose existence has no beginning and no end. He proves His existence for His creatures and for the birth of His creatures upon His eternal nature and He is needful of comparison with something in order that the creatures may be guided to Him. His signs are witnesses for His power. His being cannot be described and visions cannot perceive Him and thoughts cannot reach upto His being. He is such that nothing is like him and He is the hearer and seer. And I testify that there is no God except Allah the One without any partner. He has promised His rewards for His obedience and His punishment for His disobedience. And I testify that Muhammad (s.a.w.s) is His servant and His Messenger. He sent him with the illuminated and clear book and assured that falsehood will not approach it from front or back. It is the revelation of the Wise and the Praised Lord. And I testify that Amirul Momineen Ali Ibne Abi Talib (a.s.) and the Purified Imams from his progeny are the Proofs of  Allah upon His creatures after the conclusion of revelations. And I testify that their partisans (Shias) and their devotees who follow their practice and manners are on the straight path and they are the true believers and one who opposes them and leaves their path and abandons their commands and does not follow their practice has deviated from the straight path and gone astray from the way. I beseech Allah that He makes us firm on their religion, their devotion and their love and not make our hearts deviated after being guided. And that He bestows us with His bestowals, indeed He is the best of the Givers. Says Ash-Shaykh Abu Ja’far Muhammad bin Ali bin alHusain bin Musa bin Babawahy al-Qummi (r.a): That which led me to write this book was the saying of the Holy Prophet (s.a.w.s): “One who reminds (people) to do good deeds is like one who performs them himself.” I have titled this book as “Thawaabul A’amaal” and I implore the Almighty not to keep me deprived of its rewards. I have not desired anything by writing it except rewards of Almighty and to achieve His pleasure, glorified be He. And I do not intend by it anything else. And there is no power and strength except for Allah. And He is sufficient for us and the best of the trustees.

reference.citation: Thawāb al-Aʿmāl wa ʿiqāb al-Aʿmāl, Ch. 1, H. 1
reference.chapter: Introduction
reference.location: Introduction › Introduction
reference.ar_citation: ثواب الأعمال وعقاب الأعمال، باب ١، ح ١

hadiths.chapter: 1. Reward for saying: There is no God except Allah (Laa ilaaha illallaah)
hadiths.ar: حَدَّثَنَا مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ عَلِیِّ بْنِ الْحُسَیْنِ بْنِ مُوسَی بْنِ بَابَوَیْهِ الْقُمِّیُّ الْفَقِیهُ مُصَنِّفُ هَذَا الْکِتَابِ رَضِیَ اللَّهُ عَنْهُ قَالَ حَدَّثَنِی أَبِی ره قَالَ حَدَّثَنِی سَعْدُ بْنُ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ قَالَ حَدَّثَنِی أَحْمَدُ بْنُ هِلَالٍ عَنْ أَحْمَدَ بْنِ صَالِحٍ عَنْ عِیسَی بْنِ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ مِنْ وُلْدِ عُمَرَ بْنِ عَلِیٍّ عَنْ أَبِیهِ عَنْ أَبِی سَعِیدٍ الْخُدْرِیِّ عَنِ النَّبِیِّ ص قَالَ قَالَ اللَّهُ جَلَّ جَلَالُهُ لِمُوسَی بْنِ عِمْرَانَ یَا مُوسَی لَوْ أَنَّ السَّمَوَاتِ وَ عَامِرِیهِنَّ عِنْدِی وَ الْأَرَضِینَ السَّبْعَ فِی کِفَّهٍ وَ لَا إِلَهَ إِلَّا اللَّهُ فِی کِفَّهٍ مَالَتْ بِهِنَّ لَا إِلَهَ إِلَّا اللَّهُ.
hadiths.en: 1.  The Messenger of Allah (s.a.w.s.) said: Allah, the Mighty and  Sublime  told  Musa  Ibne  Imran:  O  Musa  If  the  seven heavens and the seven earths and whatever is between them are placed on one side of the balance and on the other side “Laa ilaaha illallaah”, the latter shall be found heavier.

reference.citation: Thawāb al-Aʿmāl wa ʿiqāb al-Aʿmāl, Ch. 1, H. 2
reference.chapter: Reward for saying: There is no God except Allah (Laa ilaaha illallaah)
reference.location: Rewards › Reward for saying: There is no God except Allah (Laa ilaaha illallaah)
reference.ar_citation: ثواب الأعمال وعقاب الأعمال، باب ١، ح ٢

hadiths.chapter: 1. Reward for saying: There is no God except Allah (Laa ilaaha illallaah)
hadiths.ar: أَبِی ره قَالَ حَدَّثَنِی سَعْدُ بْنُ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ عَنْ أَحْمَدَ بْنِ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ عِیسَی عَنِ الْحُسَیْنِ بْنِ سَیْفٍ عَنْ أَخِیهِ الْحَسَنِ عَنْ أَخِیهِ عَلِیٍّ عَنْ أَبِیهِ قَالَ حَدَّثَنِی الْحَجَّاجُ بْنُ أَرْطَاهَ قَالَ حَدَّثَنِی أَبُو الزُّبَیْرِ عَنْ جَابِرِ بْنِ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ عَنِ النَّبِیِّ ص قَالَ: الْمُوجِبَتَانِ قَالَ مَنْ مَاتَ یَشْهَدُ أَنْ لَا إِلَهَ إِلَّا اللَّهُ دَخَلَ الْجَنَّهَ وَ مَنْ مَاتَ یُشْرِکُ بِاللَّهِ دَخَلَ النَّارَ.
hadiths.en: 2. My father (rah) said: it was narrated to me by Sa’d b. Abdillah from Ahmad b. Muhammad b. Isa from al-Hussain b. Sayf from his brother al-Hassan from his brother Ali from his father who said it was narrated to me by al-Hajjaj b. Artah who said it was narrated to me by Abu al-Zubayr from Jabir b. Abdillah from The Prophet (s.a.w.s.) who said: Two things are causes of two others. One who dies testifying that there is no God except Allah shall enter Paradise and one who dies associating someone or something with Allah shall enter Fire (of Hell).

reference.citation: Thawāb al-Aʿmāl wa ʿiqāb al-Aʿmāl, Ch. 1, H. 3
reference.chapter: Reward for saying: There is no God except Allah (Laa ilaaha illallaah)
reference.location: Rewards › Reward for saying: There is no God except Allah (Laa ilaaha illallaah)
reference.ar_citation: ثواب الأعمال وعقاب الأعمال، باب ١، ح ٣

hadiths.chapter: 1. Reward for saying: There is no God except Allah (Laa ilaaha illallaah)
hadiths.ar: وَ بِهَذَا الْإِسْنَادِ عَنِ الْحُسَیْنِ بْنِ سَیْفٍ عَنْ أَبِیهِ عَنْ عُمَرَ بْنِ شِمْرٍ عَنْ جَابِرٍ عَنْ أَبِی جَعْفَرٍ ع قَالَ قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ص لَقِّنُوا مَوْتَاکُمْ لَا إِلَهَ إِلَّا اللَّهُ فَإِنَّهَا تَهْدِمُ الذُّنُوبَ فَقَالُوا یَا رَسُولَ اللَّهِ فَمَنْ قَالَ فِی صِحَّتِهِ فَقَالَ ذَاکَ أَهْدَمُ وَ أَهْدَمُ إِنَّ لَا إِلَهَ إِلَّا اللَّهُ أُنْسٌ لِلْمُؤْمِنِ فِی حَیَاتِهِ وَ عِنْدَ مَوْتِهِ وَ حِینَ یُبْعَثُ.
hadiths.en: 3.  The  Messenger  of  Allah  (s.a.w.s.)  said:  Prompt  your departed ones to say: ‘There is no god except Allah’ for it erases  the  sins.  So  they  asked:  O  Messenger  of  Allah (s.a.w.s.),  what  if  one  recites  this  when  one  is  healthy (alive)? The Holy Prophet (s.a.w.s.) replied, “This (utterance) destroys  the  sins  and  finishes  them  off  in  any  condition. Indeed  “There  is  no  God  except  Allah”  is  a  friend  and helper of man in life in death and in raising after death. The Messenger  of  Allah  (s.a.w.s.)  also  said:  Jibraeel  said:  O Muhammad! You shall see them when they are raised with shining countenances those who used to chant: There is no God except Allah and Allah is the greatest.” And those shall be black faced who used to say: “Woe be to you, you are ruined!”

reference.citation: Thawāb al-Aʿmāl wa ʿiqāb al-Aʿmāl, Ch. 1, H. 4
reference.chapter: Reward for saying: There is no God except Allah (Laa ilaaha illallaah)
reference.location: Rewards › Reward for saying: There is no God except Allah (Laa ilaaha illallaah)
reference.ar_citation: ثواب الأعمال وعقاب الأعمال، باب ١، ح ٤

hadiths.chapter: 1. Reward for saying: There is no God except Allah (Laa ilaaha illallaah)
hadiths.ar: وَ بِهَذَا الْإِسْنَادِ عَنِ الْحُسَیْنِ بْنِ سَیْفٍ عَنْ أَبِیهِ عَنْ عَمْرِو بْنِ جُمَیْعٍ رَفَعَهُ إِلَی النَّبِیِّ ص قَالَ: ثَمَنُ الْجَنَّهِ لَا إِلَهَ إِلَّا اللَّهُ.
hadiths.en: 4. And from the previous chain from al-Hussain b. Sayf from his father from ’Amru b. Jamee’ who raised it to The Prophet (s.a.w.s.) who said: The price of Paradise is Laa ilaaha illallaah (there is no God except Allah).

reference.citation: Thawāb al-Aʿmāl wa ʿiqāb al-Aʿmāl, Ch. 1, H. 5
reference.chapter: Reward for saying: There is no God except Allah (Laa ilaaha illallaah)
reference.location: Rewards › Reward for saying: There is no God except Allah (Laa ilaaha illallaah)
reference.ar_citation: ثواب الأعمال وعقاب الأعمال، باب ١، ح ٥

hadiths.chapter: 1. Reward for saying: There is no God except Allah (Laa ilaaha illallaah)
hadiths.ar: أَبِی ره قَالَ حَدَّثَنِی سَعْدُ بْنُ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ عَنْ أَحْمَدَ بْنِ هِلَالٍ عَنِ الْفَضْلِ بْنِ عَبْدِ الْوَهَّابِ عَنْ إِسْحَاقَ بْنِ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ عَنْ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ بْنِ الْوَلِیدِ رَفَعَهُ قَالَ قَالَ النَّبِیُّ ص مَنْ قَالَ لَا إِلَهَ إِلَّا اللَّهُ غُرِسَتْ لَهُ شَجَرَهٌ فِی الْجَنَّهِ مِنْ یَاقُوتَهٍ حَمْرَاءَ مَنْبِتُهَا فِی مِسْکٍ أَبْیَضَ أَحْلَی مِنَ الْعَسَلِ وَ أَشَدَّ بَیَاضاً مِنَ الثَّلْجِ وَ أَطْیَبَ رِیحاً مِنَ الْمِسْکِ فِیهَا ثِمَارٌ أَمْثَالُ أَثْدَاءِ الْأَبْکَارِ تَفْلِقُ عَنْ سَبْعِینَ حُلَّهً.
hadiths.en: 5.  The  Messenger  of  Allah  (s.a.w.s.)  said:  One  who  says ‘There is no god except Allah,’ for him a tree of rubies is planted  in  Paradise  which  is  sweeter  than  honey,  whiter than snow and more fragrant than Musk. And its fruits are like  breasts  of  virgins  which  are  prominent  even  from beneath seventy veils.

reference.citation: Thawāb al-Aʿmāl wa ʿiqāb al-Aʿmāl, Ch. 1, H. 6
reference.chapter: Reward for saying: There is no God except Allah (Laa ilaaha illallaah)

hadiths.chapter: 1. Reward for saying: There is no God except Allah (Laa ilaaha illallaah)
hadiths.ar: أَبِی ره قَالَ حَدَّثَنِی سَعْدُ بْنُ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ قَالَ حَدَّثَنَا أَحْمَدُ بْنُ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ عِیسَی وَ إِبْرَاهِیمُ بْنُ هَاشِمٍ وَ الْحَسَنُ بْنُ عَلِیٍّ الْکُوفِیُّ عَنِ الْحُسَیْنِ بْنِ سَیْفٍ عَنْ عُمَرَ بْنِ شِمْرٍ عَنْ جَابِرِ بْنِ یَزِیدَ الْجُعْفِیِّ عَنْ أَبِی جَعْفَرٍ ع قَالَ قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ص لَیْسَ شَیْ ءٌ إِلَّا وَ لَهُ شَیْ ءٌ یَعْدِلُهُ إِلَّا اللَّهَ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ فَإِنَّهُ لَا یَعْدِلُهُ شَیْ ءٌ وَ لَا إِلَهَ إِلَّا اللَّهُ فَإِنَّهُ لَا یَعْدِلُهَا شَیْ ءٌ وَ دَمْعَهٌ مِنْ خَوْفِ اللَّهِ فَإِنَّهُ لَیْسَ لَهَا مِثَالٌ فَإِنْ سَالَتْ عَلَی وَجْهِهِ لَمْ یَرْهَقْهُ قَتَرٌ وَ لا ذِلَّهٌ بَعْدَهَا أَبَداً.
hadiths.en: 6.  Imam Muhammad Baqir (a.s.) says that the Messenger of Allah (s.a.w.s.) said: There is nothing which is not equal in weight  to  something  else.  Except  for  Allah,  as  nothing  is equal  to  Him  and  nothing  is  equal  to  ‘There  is  no  god except Allah’ also. And there is no measure for tears that flow in fear of Allah. Even if a single tear flows on the face of  a  person,  he  or  she  would  never  be  helpless  and humiliated.

reference.citation: Thawāb al-Aʿmāl wa ʿiqāb al-Aʿmāl, Ch. 1, H. 7
reference.chapter: Reward for saying: There is no God except Allah (Laa ilaaha illallaah)
reference.location: Rewards › Reward for saying: There is no God except Allah (Laa ilaaha illallaah)
reference.ar_citation: ثواب الأعمال وعقاب الأعمال، باب ١، ح ٧

hadiths.chapter: 1. Reward for saying: There is no God except Allah (Laa ilaaha illallaah)
hadiths.ar: وَ بِهَذَا الْإِسْنَادِ عَنْ جَابِرٍ عَنْ أَبِی الطُّفَیْلِ عَنْ عَلِیٍّ صَلَوَاتُ اللَّهِ عَلَیْهِ قَالَ: مَا مِنْ عَبْدٍ مُسْلِمٍ یَقُولُ لَا إِلَهَ إِلَّا اللَّهُ إِلَّا صَعِدَتْ تَخْرِقُ کُلَّ سَقْفٍ لَا تَمُرُّ بِشَیْ ءٍ مِنْ سَیِّئَاتِهِ إِلَّا طَلَسَتْهَا حَتَّی تَنْتَهِیَ إِلَی مِثْلِهَا مِنَ الْحَسَنَاتِ فَتَقِفَ.
hadiths.en: 7. And from the previous chain from Jabir from Abi al-Tufayl from Ali (a.s.) who said: ‘There is no God except Allah’ and his or her grades are not elevated. He or she will continue to scale all heights. None of his (or her) sins shall be questioned. And for each of his sins equal number of good deeds would be given and he would be satisfied.

reference.citation: Thawāb al-Aʿmāl wa ʿiqāb al-Aʿmāl, Ch. 1, H. 8
reference.chapter: Reward for saying: There is no God except Allah (Laa ilaaha illallaah)
reference.location: Rewards › Reward for saying: There is no God except Allah (Laa ilaaha illallaah)
reference.ar_citation: ثواب الأعمال وعقاب الأعمال، باب ١، ح ٨

hadiths.chapter: 1. Reward for saying: There is no God except Allah (Laa ilaaha illallaah)
hadiths.ar: حَدَّثَنِی مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ الْحَسَنِ قَالَ حَدَّثَنِی سَعْدُ بْنُ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ عَنْ أَحْمَدَ بْنِ هِلَالٍ عَنِ ابْنِ فَضَّالٍ عَنْ أَبِی حَمْزَهَ قَالَ سَمِعْتُ أَبَا جَعْفَرٍ ع یَقُولُ مَا مِنْ شَیْ ءٍ أَعْظَمَ ثَوَاباً مِنْ شَهَادَهِ أَنْ لَا إِلَهَ إِلَّا اللَّهُ لِأَنَّ اللَّهَ تَعَالَی لَا یَعْدِلُهُ شَیْ ءٌ وَ لَا یَشْرَکُهُ فِی الْأَمْرِ أَحَدٌ.
hadiths.en: 8.  Imam Muhammad Baqir (a.s.) says: There is nothing more rewarding  than  the  testimony  of  ‘There  is  no  God  except Allah’ because Allah has not appointed anything equal to it and there is no partner of Allah in this matter.

reference.citation: Thawāb al-Aʿmāl wa ʿiqāb al-Aʿmāl, Ch. 1, H. 9

reference.ar_citation: ثواب الأعمال وعقاب الأعمال، باب ١، ح ٩

hadiths.chapter: 1. Reward for saying: There is no God except Allah (Laa ilaaha illallaah)
hadiths.ar: أَبِی ره قَالَ حَدَّثَنِی سَعْدُ بْنُ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ عَنْ أَحْمَدَ بْنِ أَبِی عَبْدِ اللَّهِ قَالَ حَدَّثَنِی أَبُو عِمْرَانَ الْعِجْلِیُّ قَالَ حَدَّثَنَا مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ سِنَانٍ قَالَ حَدَّثَنَا أَبُو الْعَلَاءِ الْخَفَّافُ قَالَ حَدَّثَنَا عَطِیَّهُ الْعَوْفِیُّ عَنْ أَبِی سَعِیدٍ الْخُدْرِیِّ قَالَ قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ص مَا قُلْتُ وَ لَا قَالَ الْقَائِلُونَ قَبْلِی مِثْلَ لَا إِلَهَ إِلَّا اللَّهُ.
hadiths.en: 9.  The  Messenger  of  Allah  (s.a.w.s.)  says:  Till  date,  I  and those who preceded me never said anything equal to ‘There is no God except Allah’.

reference.citation: Thawāb al-Aʿmāl wa ʿiqāb al-Aʿmāl, Ch. 1, H. 10
reference.chapter: Reward for saying: There is no God except Allah (Laa ilaaha illallaah)
reference.location: Rewards › Reward for saying: There is no God except Allah (Laa ilaaha illallaah)
reference.ar_citation: ثواب الأعمال وعقاب الأعمال، باب ١، ح ١٠

hadiths.chapter: 1. Reward for saying: There is no God except Allah (Laa ilaaha illallaah)
hadiths.ar: حَدَّثَنِی مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ عَلِیِّ بْنِ مَاجِیلَوَیْهِ عَنْ عَلِیِّ بْنِ إِبْرَاهِیمَ عَنْ أَبِیهِ عَنِ النَّوْفَلِیِّ عَنِ السَّکُونِیِّ عَنْ أَبِی عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ع عَنْ آبَائِهِ ع قَالَ قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ص خَیْرُ الْعِبَادَهِ قَوْلُ لَا إِلَهَ إِلَّا اللَّهُ.
hadiths.en: 10.  Imam Ja’far Sadiq (a.s.) narrates from his forefathers that the Messenger of Allah (s.a.w.s.) said: (Saying) ‘There is no God except Allah’ is the best worship act.

reference.citation: Thawāb al-Aʿmāl wa ʿiqāb al-Aʿmāl, Ch. 1, H. 11
reference.chapter: Reward for saying: There is no God except Allah (Laa ilaaha illallaah)
reference.location: Rewards › Reward for saying: There is no God except Allah (Laa ilaaha illallaah)
reference.ar_citation: ثواب الأعمال وعقاب الأعمال، باب ١، ح ١١

hadiths.chapter: 1. Reward for saying: There is no God except Allah (Laa ilaaha illallaah)
hadiths.ar: حَدَّثَنِی مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ مُوسَی بْنِ الْمُتَوَکِّلِ قَالَ حَدَّثَنِی عَلِیُّ بْنُ الْحُسَیْنِ السَّعْدَآبَادِیُّ عَنْ أَحْمَدَ بْنِ أَبِی عَبْدِ اللَّهِ قَالَ حَدَّثَنِی أَبُو عِمْرَانَ الْعِجْلِیُّ رَفَعَهُ قَالَ قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ص مَا مِنْ مُؤْمِنٍ یَقُولُ لَا إِلَهَ إِلَّا اللَّهُ إِلَّا مَحَتْ مَا فِی صَحِیفَتِهِ مِنْ سَیِّئَاتٍ حَتَّی تَنْتَهِیَ إِلَی مِثْلِهَا مِنْ حَسَنَاتٍ.
hadiths.en: 11.  The Messenger of Allah (s.a.w.s.) says: Sins are erased from the scroll of deeds of every believer who says: ‘There is no God except Allah’ and good deeds equal to the number of sins are bestowed on him.

reference.citation: Thawāb al-Aʿmāl wa ʿiqāb al-Aʿmāl, Ch. 1, H. 12
reference.chapter: Reward for saying: There is no God except Allah (Laa ilaaha illallaah)
reference.location: Rewards › Reward for saying: There is no God except Allah (Laa ilaaha illallaah)
reference.ar_citation: ثواب الأعمال وعقاب الأعمال، باب ١، ح ١٢

hadiths.chapter: 1. Reward for saying: There is no God except Allah (Laa ilaaha illallaah)
hadiths.ar: حَدَّثَنِی مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ الْحَسَنِ قَالَ حَدَّثَنِی مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ الْحَسَنِ الصَّفَّارُ عَنِ الْبَرْقِیِّ عَنِ الْحُسَیْنِ بْنِ سَیْفٍ عَنْ أَخِیهِ الْحَسَنِ عَنْ مُفَضَّلِ بْنِ صَالِحٍ عَنْ عُبَیْدِ بْنِ زُرَارَهَ قَالَ قَالَ أَبُو عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ع قَوْلُ لَا إِلَهَ إِلَّا اللَّهُ ثَمَنُ الْجَنَّهِ.
hadiths.en: 12.  Imam Ja’far Sadiq (a.s.) says, “The statement: Laa ilaaha illallaah is equal to the cost of Paradise.”

reference.citation: Thawāb al-Aʿmāl wa ʿiqāb al-Aʿmāl, Ch. 1, H. 13
reference.chapter: Reward for saying: There is no God except Allah (Laa ilaaha illallaah)
reference.location: Rewards › Reward for saying: There is no God except Allah (Laa ilaaha illallaah)
reference.ar_citation: ثواب الأعمال وعقاب الأعمال، باب ١، ح ١٣

hadiths.chapter: 1. Reward for saying: There is no God except Allah (Laa ilaaha illallaah)
hadiths.ar: وَ بِهَذَا الْإِسْنَادِ عَنْ أَحْمَدَ عَنِ الْحَسَنِ بْنِ عَلِیِّ بْنِ یَقْطِینٍ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ سِنَانٍ عَنْ حَمَّادِ بْنِ عُثْمَانَ وَ خَلَفِ بْنِ حَمَّادٍ جَمِیعاً عَنْ رِبْعِیٍّ عَنْ فُضَیْلٍ قَالَ سَمِعْتُهُ یَقُولُ أَکْثِرُوا مِنَ التَّهْلِیلِ وَ التَّکْبِیرِ فَإِنَّهُ لَیْسَ شَیْ ءٌ أَحَبَّ إِلَی اللَّهِ مِنَ التَّکْبِیرِ وَ التَّهْلِیلِ.
hadiths.en: 13.  Imam Ja’far Sadiq (a.s.) says, “Recite Tahleel (Laa ilaaha illallaah)  and takbeer (Allaahu  Akbar)  often because Allah likes nothing more than Takbeer and Tahleel.”

reference.citation: Thawāb al-Aʿmāl wa ʿiqāb al-Aʿmāl, Ch. 1, H. 14
reference.chapter: Reward for saying: There is no God except Allah (Laa ilaaha illallaah)
reference.location: Rewards › Reward for saying: There is no God except Allah (Laa ilaaha illallaah)
reference.ar_citation: ثواب الأعمال وعقاب الأعمال، باب ١، ح ١٤

hadiths.chapter: 2. Reward of reciting Laa ilaaha illallaah a hundred times
hadiths.ar: أَبِی ره قَالَ حَدَّثَنِی سَعْدُ بْنُ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ عَنْ أَحْمَدَ بْنِ أَبِی عَبْدِ اللَّهِ عَنْ أَحْمَدَ بْنِ أَبِی عُمَیْرٍ عَنْ هِشَامِ بْنِ سَالِمٍ وَ أَبِی أَیُّوبَ قَالَ قَالَ أَبُو عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ع مَنْ قَالَ لَا إِلَهَ إِلَّا اللَّهُ مِائَهَ مَرَّهٍ کَانَ أَفْضَلَ النَّاسِ ذَلِکَ الْیَوْمَ عَمَلًا إِلَّا مَنْ زَادَ.
hadiths.en: 1.  Imam  Ja’far  Sadiq  (a.s.)  says,  “The  deed  of  one  who recites Laa ilaaha illallaah a hundred times is better than all except one who recites it more than a hundred times.”

reference.citation: Thawāb al-Aʿmāl wa ʿiqāb al-Aʿmāl, Ch. 2, H. 15
reference.chapter: Reward of reciting Laa ilaaha illallaah a hundred times
reference.location: Rewards › Reward of reciting Laa ilaaha illallaah a hundred times
reference.ar_citation: ثواب الأعمال وعقاب الأعمال، باب ٢، ح ١٥

hadiths.chapter: 2. Reward of reciting Laa ilaaha illallaah a hundred times
hadiths.ar: أَبِی ره قَالَ حَدَّثَنَا سَعْدُ بْنُ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ عَنْ أَحْمَدَ بْنِ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ عِیسَی عَنِ الْحُسَیْنِ بْنِ سَیْفٍ عَنْ سَالِمِ بْنِ غَانِمٍ عَنْ أَبِی عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ع قَالَ: مَنْ قَالَ حِینَ یَأْوِی إِلَی فِرَاشِهِ لَا إِلَهَ إِلَّا اللَّهُ مِائَهَ مَرَّهٍ بَنَی اللَّهُ لَهُ بَیْتاً فِی الْجَنَّهِ وَ مَنِ اسْتَغْفَرَ حِینَ یَأْوِی إِلَی فِرَاشِهِ مِائَهَ مَرَّهٍ تَحَاتَّتْ ذُنُوبُهُ کَمَا یَسْقُطُ وَرَقُ الشَّجَرِ.
hadiths.en: 2. My father (rah) said: it was narrated to us by Sa’d b. Abdillah from Ahmad b. Muhammad b. Isa from al-Hussain b. Sayf from Salim b. Ghanim from Abi Abdillah (a.s.) who said: “Allah builds a house in Paradise for one who recites Laa ilaaha illallaah a hundred times before going to bed. And the sins of one who recites Astaghfirullah a hundred times before going to bed are destroyed in the same way as dry leaves fall off from a tree.”

reference.citation: Thawāb al-Aʿmāl wa ʿiqāb al-Aʿmāl, Ch. 2, H. 16
reference.chapter: Reward of reciting Laa ilaaha illallaah a hundred times
reference.location: Rewards › Reward of reciting Laa ilaaha illallaah a hundred times
reference.ar_citation: ثواب الأعمال وعقاب الأعمال، باب ٢، ح ١٦

hadiths.chapter: 3. Reward of reciting Laa ilaaha illallaah Wah’dahu Wah’dahu Wah’dahu
hadiths.ar: أَبِی ره قَالَ حَدَّثَنَا سَعْدُ بْنُ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ عَنْ أَحْمَدَ بْنِ مُحَمَّدٍ وَ إِبْرَاهِیمَ بْنِ هَاشِمٍ وَ الْحَسَنِ بْنِ عَلِیٍّ الْکُوفِیِّ عَنِ الْحُسَیْنِ بْنِ سَیْفٍ عَنْ أَخِیهِ عَلِیٍّ عَنْ أَبِیهِ عَنْ عَمْرِو بْنِ شِمْرٍ عَنْ جَابِرِ بْنِ یَزِیدَ الْجُعْفِیِّ عَنْ أَبِی جَعْفَرٍ ع قَالَ: جَاءَ جَبْرَئِیلُ إِلَی رَسُولِ اللَّهِ ص فَقَالَ یَا مُحَمَّدُ طُوبَی لِمَنْ قَالَ مِنْ أُمَّتِکَ لَا إِلَهَ إِلَّا اللَّهُ وَحْدَهُ وَحْدَهُ وَحْدَهُ.
hadiths.en: 1. My father (rah) said: it was narrated to us by Sa’d b. Abdillah from Ahmad b. Muhammad and Ibrahim b. Hashim and al-Hassan b. Ali al-Kufi from al-Hussain b. Sayf from his brother Ali from his father from ’Amru b. Shimr from Jabir b. Yazid al-Ju’fi from Abi Ja’far (a.s.) who said: ‘If a person of your Ummah recites Laa ilaaha illallaah Wah’dahu Wah’dahu Wah’dahu he will have Tooba (good fortune).”

reference.citation: Thawāb al-Aʿmāl wa ʿiqāb al-Aʿmāl, Ch. 3, H. 17
reference.chapter: Reward of reciting Laa ilaaha illallaah Wah’dahu Wah’dahu Wah’dahu
reference.location: Rewards › Reward of reciting Laa ilaaha illallaah Wah’dahu Wah’dahu Wah’dahu
reference.ar_citation: ثواب الأعمال وعقاب الأعمال، باب ٣، ح ١٧

hadiths.chapter: 4. Reward of reciting Laa ilaaha illallaah with sincerity
hadiths.ar: أَبِی ره قَالَ حَدَّثَنَا سَعْدُ بْنُ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ عَنْ یَعْقُوبَ بْنِ یَزِیدَ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ أَبِی عُمَیْرٍ عَنْ مُحَمَّدِ بْنِ حُمْرَانَ عَنْ أَبِی عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ع قَالَ: مَنْ قَالَ لَا إِلَهَ إِلَّا اللَّهُ مُخْلِصاً دَخَلَ الْجَنَّهَ وَ إِخْلَاصُهُ بِهَا أَنْ یَحْجُزَهُ لَا إِلَهَ إِلَّا اللَّهُ عَمَّا حَرَّمَ اللَّهُ.
hadiths.en: 1. My father (rah) said: it was narrated to us by Sa’d b. Abdillah from Ya’qub b. Yazid from Muhammad b. Abi ’Umayr from Muhammad b. Hamran from Abi Abdillah (a.s.) who said: “A person who recites Laa ilaaha illallaah with pure intention will enter Paradise and reciting Laa ilaaha illallaah with pure intention implies that he keeps away from that, which Allah has prohibited for him.”

reference.citation: Thawāb al-Aʿmāl wa ʿiqāb al-Aʿmāl, Ch. 4, H. 18
reference.chapter: Reward of reciting Laa ilaaha illallaah with sincerity
reference.location: Rewards › Reward of reciting Laa ilaaha illallaah with sincerity
reference.ar_citation: ثواب الأعمال وعقاب الأعمال، باب ٤، ح ١٨

hadiths.chapter: 4. Reward of reciting Laa ilaaha illallaah with sincerity
hadiths.ar: حَدَّثَنِی مُحَمَّدُ بْنُ مُوسَی بْنِ الْمُتَوَکِّلِ قَالَ حَدَّثَنِی عَلِیُّ بْنُ الْحُسَیْنِ السَّعْدَآبَادِیُّ عَنْ أَحْمَدَ بْنِ أَبِی عَبْدِ اللَّهِ عَنِ الْحُسَیْنِ بْنِ مَحْبُوبٍ عَنْ أَبِی جَمِیلَهَ عَنْ جَابِرٍ عَنْ أَبِی جَعْفَرٍ ع قَالَ قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ص أَتَانِی جَبْرَئِیلُ ع بَیْنَ الصَّفَا وَ الْمَرْوَهِ فَقَالَ یَا مُحَمَّدُ طُوبَی لِمَنْ قَالَ مِنْ أُمَّتِکَ لَا إِلَهَ إِلَّا اللَّهُ مُخْلِصاً.
hadiths.en: 2.  The Messenger of Allah (s.a.w.s) says, “Jibraeel (a.s.) came to  me  while  I  was  between  Safa  and  Marwa  and  said, ‘Whoever from your Ummah recites Laa ilaaha illallaah with pure intention will go to Paradise’.”

reference.citation: Thawāb al-Aʿmāl wa ʿiqāb al-Aʿmāl, Ch. 4, H. 19
reference.chapter: Reward of reciting Laa ilaaha illallaah with sincerity
reference.location: Rewards › Reward of reciting Laa ilaaha illallaah with sincerity
reference.ar_citation: ثواب الأعمال وعقاب الأعمال، باب ٤، ح ١٩

hadiths.chapter: 4. Reward of reciting Laa ilaaha illallaah with sincerity
hadiths.ar: أَبِی ره قَالَ حَدَّثَنِی سَعْدُ بْنُ عَبْدِ اللَّهِ عَنْ أَحْمَدَ بْنِ مُحَمَّدٍ وَ الْحَسَنِ بْنِ عَلِیٍّ الْکُوفِیِّ وَ إِبْرَاهِیمَ بْنِ هَاشِمٍ کُلِّهِمْ عَنِ الْحُسَیْنِ بْنِ سَیْفٍ عَنْ سُلَیْمَانَ بْنِ عُمَرَ عَنْ مُهَاجِرِ بْنِ الْحَسَنِ عَنْ زَیْدِ بْنِ أَرْقَمَ عَنِ النَّبِیِّ ص قَالَ: مَنْ قَالَ لَا إِلَهَ إِلَّا اللَّهُ مُخْلِصاً دَخَلَ الْجَنَّهَ وَ إِخْلَاصُهُ بِهَا أَنْ یَحْجُزَهُ لَا إِلَهَ إِلَّا اللَّهُ عَمَّا حَرَّمَ اللَّهُ.
hadiths.en: 3. My father (rah) said: it was narrated to me by Sa’d b. Abdillah from Ahmad b. Muhammad and al-Hassan b. Ali al-Kufi and Ibrahim b. Hashimall of themfrom al-Hussain b. Sayf from Sulayman b. Umar from Muhajir b. al-Hassan from Zayd b. Arqam from The Prophet (s.a.w.s.) who said: “A person who recites Laa ilaaha illallaah with pure intention will enter Paradise. And reciting it with pure intention implies that he keeps away from what has been prohibited by Allah.”

reference.citation: Thawāb al-Aʿmāl wa ʿiqāb al-Aʿmāl, Ch. 4, H. 20
reference.chapter: Reward of reciting Laa ilaaha illallaah with sincerity
reference.location: Rewards › Reward of reciting Laa ilaaha illallaah with sincerity
reference.ar_citation: ثواب الأعمال وعقاب الأعمال، باب ٤، ح ٢٠
